title: Carex basutorum
taxonomy: Carex basutorum is a species of sedge that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. The genus Carex is a large and diverse group of plants, with over 2,000 species worldwide. Carex basutorum is native to southern Africa and is commonly found in moist, grassy areas. This species is often used in wetland restoration and conservation efforts due to its ability to thrive in a variety of conditions. Carex basutorum is classified as a perennial plant, which means it grows back year after year from the same roots.

intro: Carex basutorum, also known as the Mountain Kaffirs reed sedge, is a perennial plant species native to the mountains of eastern South Africa. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can aid in reducing pain and inflammation, as well as protecting against oxidative stress and cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Carex basutorum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
